sourceMovie.title: Sex Is Zero
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Sex Is Zero (2002) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. JK Youn's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: High School Musical
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sex Is Zero clicked for you, High School Musical (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sex Is Zero?
faq.answer: Try High School Musical, In Her Shoes, Great Expectations—each shares DNA with Sex Is Zero while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sex Is Zero?
faq.answer: Sex Is Zero (2002) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sex Is Zero part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sex is Zero Series" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
